title: Idempotent worker using Ecto.Multi for the side-effect-and-mark-done dance
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.ChargeWorker` (queue `:billing`, max_attempts 8). perform/1 takes `%{"charge_id" => id}`. It should use an `Ecto.Multi` to: (1) load the charge with `Multi.run/3`, returning `{:error, :already_charged}` if `charge.processed_at` is set (so Multi halts), (2) call the external charge API, (3) update the charge record with `processed_at`. On a `{:error, :already_charged}` result, return `:ok` (not an error — the job was already successful). Run the Multi with `Repo.transaction/1`. Return `:ok` on success, `{:error, reason}` otherwise. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/charge_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Combines the idempotency philosophy with Ecto.Multi for transactional atomicity on the side effect itself. Multi-concept.
